title: lepidium alyssoides var. jonesii
intro: Lepidium alyssoides var. jonesii, also known as Jones' cress or mountain pepper grass, is a variety of the lepidium alyssoides plant, which belongs to the mustard family (Brassicaceae) and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Lepidium alyssoides var. jonesii has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to help treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of certain cancers. Lepidium alyssoides var. jonesii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
